fresh_facts.claim: President Trump announced that US forces will remain in Iran until Tehran agrees to a new nuclear and regional deal, linking sustained US military presence to negotiations.
Trump warned of renewed US strikes if ceasefire talks fail, stating forces are 'loading up the ships with the best ammunition.'
A US MQ-4C Triton drone disappeared over the Strait of Hormuz hours before ceasefire discussions, possibly due to hostile action.
US intelligence estimates Iran retains over 1,000 medium-range ballistic missiles intact in fortified underground sites despite prior strikes.
Operation Epic Fury destroyed 90% of Iranian weapons factories, all Shahed drone sites, 80% of the nuclear industrial base, and 20+ naval facilities.
A six-week war between the US and Iran ended with a ceasefire that Iran accepted under pressure, described as a 'pause' with US forces ready to resume operations.
Prediction markets assess odds of Iranian regime collapse by June 30, 2026, at 8.5%, down from 12% one day prior.
US-Iran ceasefire talks are scheduled to be led by VP JD Vance in Pakistan on April 11-12, 2026.
Iran claims ceasefire violations through Israeli strikes on Hezbollah in Lebanon and Iranian territory; the US denies these violations, stating the truce excludes Lebanon.
Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, and the US is demanding its reopening as part of ceasefire negotiations.
The US and Iran dispute ceasefire frameworks, with Iran proposing a 10-point plan and the US proposing a 15-point plan.
Trump warned of resumed 'real shooting' if ceasefire terms are not met while maintaining US troop presence in the Gulf.
Iran's ambassador to Pakistan announced a delegation arrival for Islamabad talks but then deleted the announcement.
Prediction markets show 99.6% confidence in ceasefire holding by April 15, 2026.
The war between the US and Iran began on February 28, 2026.
Economic shocks from the war, including Strait of Hormuz disruptions, risk prolonged oil and gas price spikes and inflation.
CSIS noted unresolved Iranian nuclear ambitions, Lebanon destabilization, and shadow war risks under the ceasefire in an April 8 analysis.
The Iranian regime faces economic turmoil, high inflation, unemployment, and rising social unrest amid weakened military capabilities.
The US conducted over 10,000 military missions hitting 13,000+ targets during the war with Iran.

regime_fall.current_status: Following devastating US military strikes in early April 2026 that degraded Iran's military capabilities by 80-95%, a ceasefire negotiated on April 10 collapsed as Israeli strikes resumed in mid-June. The regime now faces critical military losses, severe economic isolation, institutional erosion under Supreme Leader control, and widespread internal unrest despite intense security crackdowns.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Post-ceasefire repression surge including over 700 arrests, IRGC/Basij raids across Iranian cities, and fatal shootings in Khomein and Hamedan on July 2, 2026, indicating regime consolidating control through internal security operations amid military losses.
